summary: PARTIALLY_IMPLEMENTED. HEAD 545dec476ac2bce7e8c82a0a21e307ea2341874e is exactly origin/main (zero commits ahead). Existing baseline pieces include shared cost estimation/pricing-source metadata, direct Codex token/cost metadata preservation, and #2630 incident reconstruction that reports observed values or an explicit unavailable reason. However, no #2560 merge was found in recent history, docs/Observability/CostAttribution.md is absent, no canonical versioned usage-attribution schema or durable step-attempt journal/rollup is present, Omnigent has no usage normalizer, and Workflow Detail has no Usage & Cost surface. New code and documentation work is required.

requirements.description: Every new provider request can produce exactly one canonical usage record or one explicit unavailable record.
Usage records are durably keyed to workflow, run, logical step, step execution, and attempt/turn where applicable.
Direct providers, direct Codex compatibility, and Omnigent bridge sessions use the same attribution contract.

Provider-reported, bridge-reported, estimated, and unavailable values are visibly distinct. Existing chat/Responses and incident projections cover only some provider/estimated/unavailable cases; there is no shared provenance/confidence model.

Pricing source/version is preserved for every estimate and historical estimates are not silently repriced. Existing paths preserve some pricing-source metadata, but no canonical pricing-versioned durable attribution record exists.
Activity retry, stream replay, bridge reconnect, and duplicate terminal events cannot double count usage.

Failed requests with reported usage contribute to totals; unknown usage never appears as zero. Incident reconstruction represents missing observed cost as unavailable, but there is no canonical failed-request rollup across producer paths.
Workflow Detail shows workflow, step, and attempt/turn breakdowns with retry/remediation contribution.
Incident reconstruction and Workflow Detail resolve the same durable attribution data.
Aggregate metrics use only approved bounded labels and reconcile within documented tolerance to durable totals.
Authorization, redaction, retention, and invoice-disclaimer behavior are tested.
Coverage metrics identify runtime/provider paths that still cannot report usage.
